_comment: Windows (WSL2) Claude Code Settings Template (LEGACY mcpServers format)
_instructions: Copy this to ~/.claude/settings.json inside your WSL2/Ubuntu terminal. Replace op:// references with your actual 1Password paths. Replace YOURUSERNAME with your Linux username. Remove _comment and _instructions keys before using.
_legacy_note: The mcpServers block below is the legacy hand-rolled format. The recommended path is now `claude mcp add <name>` (see guides/04-mcp-servers.md). This template still works and is kept for reference, but new users should prefer the CLI command.

brave-search._comment: Get API key from brave.com/search/api — free tier available

gmail._comment: Requires Google Cloud project with Gmail API enabled. See guides/04-mcp-servers.md. Path is WSL2 Linux path.

google-calendar._comment: Uses same Google Cloud credentials as Gmail

notion._comment: Create integration at notion.so/my-integrations, then share pages with it

slack._comment: Create Slack app at api.slack.com/apps with channels:read, chat:write, channels:history scopes

filesystem._comment: Gives Claude access to specific directories. WSL2 paths shown. Your Windows C: drive is at /mnt/c/ in WSL2.

_windows_notes: All paths in this file should use Linux paths (/home/... or /mnt/c/...)
Your Windows files are accessible at /mnt/c/Users/[WindowsUsername]/
WSL2 cron runs only when WSL2 is active. For true scheduled tasks, use Windows Task Scheduler to invoke WSL2.
Claude Code runs inside WSL2 — always open your Ubuntu terminal to use it
The 1Password CLI (op) runs inside WSL2 separately from the 1Password Windows app
